<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.86) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Clarify ownership of schemas between `contracts.ts` and `policy.ts` to ensure guardrail changes don’t require concurrent hench core edits.
[observation] [info] Codify how `paths.ts` normalizes workspace directories to prevent regressions when web tooling (web/web-2) invokes shell actions.
[observation] [info] Leverage the guard entrypoints to expose a standalone lint/check command so other repos can reuse guard policies without the whole agent.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (0.86) — files are tightly interconnected
- Bidirectional imports with hench core show it acts as the policy brain for runtime safety.
- Diverse tests (commands, paths, policy) demonstrate commitment to hardening.
- Clarify ownership of schemas between `contracts.ts` and `policy.ts` to ensure guardrail changes don’t require concurrent hench core edits.
- Leverage the guard entrypoints to expose a standalone lint/check command so other repos can reuse guard policies without the whole agent.
- Codify how `paths.ts` normalizes workspace directories to prevent regressions when web tooling (web/web-2) invokes shell actions.
- [call graph] 114 internal calls, 53 outgoing, 1 incoming (cohesion: 0.68, coupling: 0.32)

</insights>
